description_long: This chair is constructed from a network of thick, cylindrical metal pipes, forming a geometric, open-frame structure. The frame is weathered, with visible rust and patina, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The seat is a simple horizontal bar, supported by a rectangular base, while the backrest is formed by two vertical bars connected to the top frame. The armrests are absent, and the overall design is utilitarian and industrial, likely intended for public or urban environments. The open sides and back give it a minimalist, skeletal appearance, and the robust construction implies durability against heavy use or vandalism.
description: A weathered, industrial-style chair made from thick, rusted metal pipes in an open-frame, geometric design. It features a simple seat bar, a rectangular base, and a minimal backrest, with no armrests.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rectangular base, vertical backrest bars, and the open, boxy frame structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the angled top frame and the depth of the seat, with the open sides clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the backrest bars and the symmetry of the frame, emphasizing the open, skeletal design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the angled top and the open, industrial frame.
